judgmentEn: Retreat. Success. In what is small, perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 天下有山，遁；君子以远小人，不恶而严。
imageEn: Mountain under heaven: the image of Retreat. Thus the superior man keeps the inferior man at a distance, not angrily but with reserve.
interpretationZh: 遁卦象征退避隐遁，天下有山，山虽高却不及天。小人势力渐长，君子宜适时退让，保全正道。
interpretationEn: Dun symbolizes retreat and withdrawal. The mountain under heaven cannot reach the sky. When inferior forces grow, the wise withdraw to preserve integrity.

lines.textZh: 初六：遁尾，厉，勿用有攸往。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: At the tail in retreat. This is dangerous. One must not wish to undertake anything.
lines.interpretationZh: 退避时落在最后，处境危险，不宜有所行动。
lines.interpretationEn: Lagging behind in retreat is dangerous. Do not undertake anything.
lines.detailZh: “遁尾，厉，勿用有攸往。”初六居遁之始，阴柔在下，如退而落后，仍恋其后尾，故有危险。此爻告诫人在应退之时，若迟疑不决、拖泥带水，最易陷入被动。此时不宜再有所前往，重要的是立刻止损、果断抽身。
lines.detailEn: “Retreat at the tail. Danger. Do not undertake anything.” The first line shows one who is late in withdrawing, still caught at the rear. Because retreat has already become necessary, hesitation creates risk. The lesson is to stop clinging and avoid new initiatives. When the time to withdraw has come, delay is what makes the situation dangerous.

lines.textZh: 六二：执之用黄牛之革，莫之胜说。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: He holds him fast with yellow oxhide. No one can tear him loose.
lines.interpretationZh: 用黄牛皮绳牢牢绑住，无人能解开，坚守不退。
lines.interpretationEn: Held fast with yellow oxhide. No one can tear him away. Firm resolve.
lines.detailZh: “执之用黄牛之革，莫之胜说。”六二中正柔顺，以黄牛革为喻，象征稳固、坚韧、守持。此爻说明退守并非松散消极，而应如用坚革系住自己，不为外界诱惑所动。能守住原则与位置，别人也无法轻易使你动摇。
lines.detailEn: “He holds fast with the hide of a yellow ox. No one can loosen it.” The second line speaks of secure restraint. The yellow ox symbolizes centrality, sincerity, and dependable strength. Retreat here means firmly holding to what is right, not being pulled into action by pressure or temptation. What is anchored in principle cannot easily be undone.

lines.textZh: 九三：系遁，有疾厉，畜臣妾吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: A halted retreat is nerve-wracking and dangerous. To retain people as men- and maidservants brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被牵绊而不能退避，有危险。蓄养仆从则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: A halted retreat is dangerous and exhausting. Retaining servants brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: “系遁，有疾厉，畜臣妾吉。”九三阳刚而处下卦之上，想退却又有所牵系，因此形成病患般的危险。说明人在退场时最怕关系、利益、情感纠缠不清。若不能完全脱身，至少应把事务收束于可控的小范围，如“畜臣妾”般处理内部人事，则尚可得吉。
lines.detailEn: “Retreat bound fast. There is illness and danger. Keeping servants and concubines brings good fortune.” The third line depicts retreat hindered by attachments. One wants to withdraw, yet remains entangled in obligations or desires. This creates strain and danger. If full retreat is impossible, one should at least limit responsibilities and manage only what is close and controllable.

lines.textZh: 九四：好遁，君子吉，小人否。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Voluntary retreat brings good fortune to the superior man and downfall to the inferior man.
lines.interpretationZh: 从容退避，君子吉利，小人则不然。
lines.interpretationEn: Voluntary retreat. The superior man finds fortune; the inferior man does not.
lines.detailZh: “好遁，君子吉，小人否。”九四已近上卦，能顺势而退，故称“好遁”。君子明白进退之义，退中有节，所以吉；小人则只把退当成失利或逃避，既舍不得利益，又无道义依据，因此不利。此爻强调退的品质取决于人格境界。
lines.detailEn: “Voluntary retreat brings good fortune to the superior person, but not to the inferior person.” The fourth line shows a graceful and willing withdrawal. For the superior person, retreat is a conscious moral choice and therefore auspicious. For the inferior person, retreat lacks inner clarity and becomes mere frustration or evasion. The line distinguishes noble withdrawal from reluctant defeat.

lines.textZh: 九五：嘉遁，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: Friendly retreat. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 美好的退避，坚守正道则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: A gracious retreat. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “嘉遁，贞吉。”九五居尊得正，是全卦最能体现遁之美者。其退并非出于懦弱，而是审时度势之后的从容选择，因此称“嘉”。能够守正而退，保其德望，不失中道，所以吉。此爻提示领导者尤需懂得在不宜硬进时体面收束。
lines.detailEn: “Admirable retreat. Persistence brings good fortune.” The fifth line is the ruler of the hexagram and represents the most exemplary form of retreat. This is dignified, measured, and fully aligned with correctness. There is no panic or shame in it. By withdrawing at the right time while remaining true to principle, one preserves honor and future possibility.

lines.textZh: 上九：肥遁，无不利。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Cheerful retreat. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 远走高飞，从容退隐，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: A cheerful, unencumbered retreat. Everything furthers.
lines.detailZh: “肥遁，无不利。”上九居遁之终，已能超然物外，无牵无累，所以退得丰厚宽裕，称为“肥遁”。这不是消瘦狼狈之退，而是精神与处境都从容的退出。能如此，则进退皆由己，不受外物挟制，故无所不利。
lines.detailEn: “Abundant retreat. Nothing is unfavorable.” The top line shows retreat brought to completion. One is no longer entangled and can withdraw freely, fully, and without loss of dignity. This “abundant retreat” suggests spaciousness rather than deprivation. When one can disengage completely and calmly, all becomes favorable because one is no longer ruled by external pressure.

overviewZh: 遁卦，上乾下艮，象为天下有山。天本欲上行，山却横陈于下，示意阳气虽盛，却当因时退避，不与浊势正面纠缠。遁不是畏惧失败，也不是消极逃亡，而是在局势不利、君子道衰、小人势长之时，保存元气、收敛锋芒、待机再起的智慧。卦辞曰“遁：亨，小利贞”，说明退让本身并非失道，只要守住正固，退中自有通达。此卦强调明辨进退：能进者固然可贵，知退者尤见成熟。真正高明的遁，是心中有定力、行动有分寸，既不被情绪驱使，也不因外界压力失去原则，在安静抽身中完成自保、蓄势与转机。
overviewEn: Hexagram 33, Dun (Retreat), is formed by Qian above and Gen below, creating the image of heaven above the mountain. Its central teaching is not cowardly escape, but timely withdrawal. When conditions turn unfavorable, when noble intentions are blocked, or when inferior forces gain temporary momentum, wisdom lies in stepping back without losing inner principle. The Judgment says, “Retreat. Success. Small benefit in persistence,” showing that retreat can still be auspicious if guided by correctness. Dun teaches discernment in timing: advance when the way is open, withdraw when contention would only waste strength. True retreat is strategic, not passive. It preserves dignity, avoids fruitless struggle, and gathers strength for a more fitting moment. The hexagram therefore speaks to restraint, self-protection, moral clarity, and the ability to disengage without bitterness. In that sense, retreat becomes an act of strength and intelligent alignment with circumstance.
judgmentDetailZh: 《彖传》曰：“遁亨，遁而亨也。刚当位而应，与时行也。小利贞，浸而长也。遁之时义大矣哉！”此言遁卦之“亨”，并非因退而失志，反而正因懂得退，方能保全通达。九五居尊位而得中正，下应六二，说明君子即使处于应退之时，也仍可凭德性与分寸维持秩序。所谓“小利贞”，是说小人之势渐长，君子不宜躁进扩张，只宜守正、固本、缓退。遁卦最重要的，不是退得快，而是退得正、退得稳、退得不失其道。若明知不可为而强争，只会自损其身；若审势而退，则可避祸养德，静待时变。故遁的深义，在于顺时而行、以退为进，把看似消极的动作化为长远布局。
judgmentDetailEn: The Tuan Commentary says: “Retreat brings success, because retreating is what makes success possible. The firm is in its proper place and responds, acting in accord with the time. Small benefit in persistence, because what is inferior is gradually growing. Great indeed is the meaning of the time of Retreat!” This explains that the success of Dun does not come in spite of withdrawal, but through it. The superior person recognizes when the climate no longer supports open advance. Firmness remains in the correct place, especially in the ruling fifth line, so retreat is not surrender of principle but adaptation to timing. The phrase “small benefit in persistence” warns that when inferior influences are rising, one should not overreach. It is enough to remain upright, preserve integrity, and reduce exposure. The greatness of Retreat lies in knowing that moral clarity sometimes requires distance rather than confrontation. Proper withdrawal prevents needless damage, protects inner strength, and prepares the ground for future action. Thus the hexagram turns retreat into a disciplined strategy aligned with time and truth.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“天下有山，遁；君子以远小人，不恶而严。”天在上，山在下，山势隆起，似与天道相隔，形成有所退避、彼此分界之象。此象告诉人们，面对不正之人、不良之风，君子未必要怒斥争斗，而应主动保持距离，立场清楚，界限分明。所谓“不恶而严”，是内心不必怀恨，不陷于情绪对抗；但在原则、礼法、态度上又必须庄重坚定，不可轻慢混同。遁卦之象因此强调“有边界的仁厚”：既不与恶同流，也不因厌恶而失了修养。山天之间的空阔，正像君子为自己保留的一段清明空间。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “There is a mountain beneath heaven: Retreat. Thus the superior person keeps inferior people at a distance, not with hatred, but with dignity.” Heaven above and mountain below create a visible separation. The image is not one of collapse, but of distance, layering, and boundary. It teaches that when a person encounters pettiness, corruption, or unhealthy influence, the wisest response is often not direct emotional combat, but composed separation. “Not with hatred, but with dignity” is the key. One need not be consumed by resentment; yet one must remain firm, clear, and principled. Retreat here is ethical spacing. It protects one’s mind, values, and direction without descending into hostility. The image suggests that healthy distance can itself be a moral act. Like the open space between mountain and sky, the superior person preserves a realm of inner clarity where integrity remains untouched by surrounding disorder.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：遁卦提示在职场或事业竞争中，若环境已被内耗、权斗、错误方向裹挟，不宜硬碰硬。适合暂避锋芒、调整岗位、沉淀能力，等待更合适的平台再发力。感情：关系中若长期失衡、压抑或价值观渐行渐远，遁并非冷酷，而是给彼此留出清醒空间。暂时抽离、减少纠缠，有时比勉强维系更成熟。财运：此卦不利冒进投机，尤其在市场混乱、信息失真时，更应保守理财、减少高风险投入，以守成为主。健康：遁也象征身心需要撤离消耗源，如过劳、人际压力、情绪透支等。此时宜休整作息、远离刺激、重建节律。总体而言，现代人的“遁”不是逃避责任，而是从无谓消耗中退出，把时间、精力与资源重新投向真正值得之处。懂得退出错误局面，本身就是一种成熟的掌控力。
modernApplicationEn: Career: Dun advises that in work or competition, if the environment has become dominated by politics, internal friction, or a fundamentally flawed direction, direct confrontation may be wasteful. It can be wiser to step back, change roles, build skill quietly, or wait for a healthier platform before advancing again. Relationships: in love, retreat does not necessarily mean indifference. If a bond has become imbalanced, suffocating, or misaligned in values, creating distance may allow clarity and preserve dignity for both people. Finance: this hexagram does not favor reckless speculation. In chaotic markets or unreliable information conditions, caution, liquidity, and disciplined spending are preferable to aggressive risk-taking. Health: retreat can also mean withdrawing from sources of depletion—overwork, toxic social settings, chronic stress, or emotional overload. Rest, boundaries, and restoring rhythm become essential. Overall, modern retreat is not running away from life; it is consciously exiting unproductive struggle so that energy, attention, and resources can be redirected toward what is sustainable, meaningful, and genuinely beneficial.
historicalStoryZh: 遁卦常使人联想到商末周初伯夷、叔齐的故事。二人身为孤竹君之子，面对王位之争已能主动退让；后来周武王伐纣，他们又因坚持自身道义，不愿食周粟，最终隐于首阳山。后人虽对其选择见仁见智，但其精神正合遁卦之旨：当时代价值与自身信念难以相容时，不以愤怒求胜，不以苟同求安，而是选择退身守志。遁不是简单逃离现实，而是在无法改变大局时，仍为内心保留最后的正直与节义。伯夷、叔齐以退全道，正体现了“远小人，不恶而严”的古典风骨。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical parallel for Hexagram 33 is the story of Boyi and Shuqi at the end of the Shang and beginning of the Zhou dynasty. As sons of the ruler of Guzhu, they were already associated with yielding rather than fighting over succession. Later, when King Wu of Zhou overthrew the Shang, they refused to endorse the new order according to their own moral understanding, and eventually withdrew to Mount Shouyang. Whether later generations praise or criticize their choice, their spirit reflects Dun: when the age moves in a direction one cannot conscientiously join, one may withdraw rather than rage, flatter, or compromise inwardly. Their retreat was not mere escape, but a preservation of principle. In this sense, they embody the hexagram’s teaching of maintaining distance from what one cannot approve, not through hatred, but through steadfast dignity.
relatedHexagramsNote: 遁与第12卦“否”都涉及君子受阻、小人渐长的时势，但否偏重闭塞不通的整体局面，遁则强调在此局中如何主动退避、自保其道。它也与第52卦“艮”相通，皆重止与守界，只是遁更重时机判断与有意识的抽身。
references: 《周易》遁卦卦辞、爻辞、彖传、象传

judgmentEn: The Power of the Great. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 雷在天上，大壮；君子以非礼弗履。
imageEn: Thunder in heaven above: the image of the Power of the Great. Thus the superior man does not tread upon paths that do not accord with established order.
interpretationZh: 大壮卦象征强大的力量，雷在天上，声势浩大。阳刚之力正盛，但须以正道约束，非礼勿行。
interpretationEn: Da Zhuang symbolizes great power. Thunder above heaven is mighty. Yang force is at its peak, but must be guided by propriety.

lines.textZh: 初九：壮于趾，征凶，有孚。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Power in the toes. Continuing brings misfortune. This is certainly true.
lines.interpretationZh: 力量在脚趾，急于前进则凶，确实如此。
lines.interpretationEn: Power in the toes. Advancing rashly brings misfortune. This is certain.
lines.detailZh: 初九曰“壮于趾，征凶，有孚”。趾在下，象征力量刚起于脚下、意气先行。此爻提示人在初得气势时，最容易还未审时度势就急于出征、急于证明自己。脚下有力，不等于全局成熟；若凭一腔刚勇贸然行动，反而有凶险。但“有孚”说明其动机未必有私，可能确有真诚与担当，只是时位未足、方式太急。因此此爻之诫，不是否定行动，而是提醒在起步阶段应先稳根基、察形势、蓄力量。真正的强者，不是刚有一点资本就向前猛冲，而是懂得先立住，再发力。
lines.detailEn: The first line says, “Power in the toes. Advancing brings misfortune. There is sincerity.” The toes symbolize force beginning at the very bottom, where eagerness moves before the whole body is ready. This line warns against acting too soon simply because strength has started to gather. Having impulse and courage does not mean the overall situation is ripe. Rash advance invites trouble. Yet the phrase “there is sincerity” suggests the motive may be genuine rather than selfish. The problem is not bad intent but premature action. At an early stage, one should consolidate position, study conditions, and build support before pressing forward. Real strength is not proven by lunging at the first opportunity; it is proven by knowing how to prepare before movement becomes decisive.

lines.textZh: 九二：贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.detailZh: 九二曰“贞吉”。此爻居中得正，刚而能守，是大壮卦中最稳健的一爻。它说明真正的强，不必喧张外露，也不必处处与人角力，只要守住正道、守住中位，自然得吉。九二之可贵，在于既有阳刚之质，又处下卦中位，不偏不激，最合“利贞”之义。现实中，这意味着当你拥有能力与机会时，不需要过度表现，更不宜被情绪驱动；只要按原则行事、保持节制与稳定，事情反而顺遂。此爻像是对全卦的一条主轴：大壮之时，最怕失中，最贵得中。能中正，则强盛成为可靠力量，而非危险压力。
lines.detailEn: The second line says simply, “Perseverance brings good fortune.” This is one of the most balanced lines in the hexagram. It occupies a central and proper place, showing strength that is disciplined rather than flamboyant. Here, power does not need display. It succeeds because it remains centered, correct, and measured. This line perfectly embodies the hexagram’s main principle: great strength is beneficial when joined to rectitude. In practical terms, it advises that when one has ability, influence, or opportunity, there is no need to force recognition or react impulsively. Steadiness, principle, and moderation are enough. The line teaches that in times of expanding power, the greatest danger is losing the center. To remain centered is to transform force into dependable effectiveness rather than oppressive intensity.

lines.textZh: 九三：小人用壮，君子用罔，贞厉。羝羊触藩，羸其角。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The inferior man works through power. The superior man does not act thus. To continue is dangerous. A goat butts against a hedge and gets its horns entangled.
lines.interpretationZh: 小人逞强用力，君子不这样做。公羊抵篱笆，角被缠住，蛮力行事有危险。
lines.interpretationEn: The inferior man uses brute force. The superior man does not. A goat butts a hedge and tangles its horns.
lines.detailZh: 九三曰“小人用壮，君子用罔，贞厉。羝羊触藩，羸其角。”此爻极具警示意味。小人得势，往往拿力量去压人、争胜、斗狠；君子虽有其壮，却不如此用，或宁可似“罔”般不以蛮力取胜。即使守正，也仍有危险，因为所处之位刚过而近躁，容易陷入进退失据。羝羊触藩的比喻尤为生动：公羊恃其角利，猛触篱笆，结果角反被卡住。现实中，此爻对应那种“我有能力，所以我硬推”的状态，看似强硬，实则被障碍反制。此爻教人：力量若失去智慧与分寸，越强越容易被困；君子之壮，贵在不逞。
lines.detailEn: The third line says, “The petty person uses power; the noble person does not use it in that way. Perseverance brings danger. A ram butts a fence and gets its horns entangled.” This is one of the clearest warnings in the hexagram. Inferior character treats strength as a tool for domination, contention, and ego. The noble person, though equally strong, refuses crude application of force. Even with correct intention, danger remains here because the position is overly hard and close to excess. The ram image is vivid: trusting its horns, it charges the barrier, only to become trapped by the very weapon it relied upon. In life, this line appears when someone thinks, “I can push this through,” and mistakes capacity for wisdom. The lesson is sharp: force without judgment creates self-entanglement. Noble strength is distinguished not by how hard it strikes, but by how well it avoids needless collision.

lines.textZh: 九四：贞吉悔亡，藩决不羸，壮于大舆之輹。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. The hedge opens; there is no entanglement. Power depends upon the axle of a big cart.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。篱笆打开不再缠绕，力量如大车之轴般稳固。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. The hedge opens. Power rests on the axle of a great cart.
lines.detailZh: 九四曰“贞吉悔亡，藩决不羸，壮于大舆之輹。”此爻由下入上，是由个人之壮转向可负大任之壮。守正则吉，过去因冒进可能生出的悔意也会消失。与九三不同，此时“藩决不羸”，篱笆已开，不再被卡，说明障碍因时机成熟与方法得当而被突破；又言“大舆之輹”，輹为车轴关键部位，象征能承重、能稳定前行的核心力量。此爻不是逞匹夫之勇，而是进入一种可支撑大局的力量状态。现实意义上，表示经过前期磨炼后，终于找到正确通道，可以承担项目、组织、责任与推进任务。关键仍是“贞”，若守原则，则力量化为建功立业之器。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says, “Perseverance brings good fortune; regret vanishes. The fence breaks open, and there is no entanglement. Power is in the axle straps of a great cart.” This marks a transition from raw force to usable strength. Unlike the previous line, the obstacle here yields instead of trapping. The image of the broken fence shows that conditions have changed: timing is better, and the method is sound. The great cart’s axle straps symbolize a load-bearing structural power—strength that supports movement and carries responsibility. This is not reckless courage but mature capability. Regret disappears because action is no longer premature or misapplied. In practical terms, the line suggests that after learning restraint, one becomes capable of advancing effectively, managing larger duties, and moving substantial undertakings forward. The continuing condition is perseverance in what is right. When principle remains intact, power becomes an instrument of real accomplishment.

lines.textZh: 六五：丧羊于易，无悔。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Loses the goat with ease. No remorse.
lines.interpretationZh: 轻易失去公羊，但无悔恨，放弃刚强反而得安。
lines.interpretationEn: The goat is lost with ease. No remorse. Letting go of stubbornness brings peace.
lines.detailZh: 六五曰“丧羊于易，无悔。”六五以柔居尊，在大壮之时显得格外高明。它不靠正面冲撞取胜，而是以柔御刚、以中驭壮。“易”可解为平易之地，也可引申为轻松自然之境；“丧羊”不是重大损失，更像主动放下某种争强之心，或在不着痕迹间化解冲突。身居高位而不争一时之锋，故能无悔。此爻说明，最上乘的强大，不一定表现为压制与占有，反而可能表现为适时放手、柔性调度、顺势化解。现实中，这是一种高位者的领导智慧：不必事事硬控，不必每局都赢，舍小取大，反而保全整体。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says, “Losing the ram in ease. No regret.” As a yielding line in a ruling place, it presents a refined form of power. Here, strength is governed from the center through softness rather than confrontation. “Ease” may suggest open ground or a situation resolved without strain. “Losing the ram” does not imply serious loss so much as releasing the impulse to contest, dominate, or insist. Because the line occupies a high and balanced position, it can afford not to win by force. Thus there is no regret. This line teaches that the highest exercise of power may involve letting go, diffusing conflict, or guiding events without visible coercion. In modern terms, it reflects mature leadership: not controlling everything harshly, not needing victory in every skirmish, and knowing how to sacrifice a lesser point to preserve the greater whole.

lines.textZh: 上六：羝羊触藩，不能退，不能遂，无攸利，艰则吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: A goat butts against a hedge. It cannot go backward, it cannot go forward. Nothing serves to further. If one notes the difficulty, this brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 公羊抵篱笆，进退两难，无所利。认识到困难则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: A goat stuck in a hedge, unable to advance or retreat. Recognizing the difficulty brings fortune.
lines.detailZh: 上六曰“羝羊触藩，不能退，不能遂，无攸利，艰则吉。”到大壮之极，最怕盛极而滞。上六已处卦终，力量用到尽头，若仍执意前冲，就会陷入“不能退，不能遂”的僵局：退不下来，进也不成，徒然消耗。此与九三同言羝羊触藩，但困境更深，因为此时不是一时莽撞，而是强势惯性已成。所幸爻辞仍留出转机：“艰则吉”，意即若能正视困难、承认受阻、放下硬拗之心，转而谨慎处理，仍可趋吉。现实中，此爻常见于项目尾声、关系僵持、组织扩张过头之后的卡顿期。解决之道不是再加码，而是先止、先缓、先解结。
lines.detailEn: The top line says, “A ram butts a fence. It cannot retreat, it cannot advance. Nothing is favorable. If one accepts the difficulty, there is good fortune.” At the peak of Great Power, excess turns into blockage. This line stands at the end of the hexagram, where force has been driven to its limit. If one still insists on pushing, the result is deadlock: unable to move forward, unable to withdraw cleanly. Compared with the third line, the entanglement here is more severe because it comes not from a single rash act, but from momentum carried too far. Still, the line offers a way out: good fortune comes through recognizing the difficulty. When one stops forcing, acknowledges the bind, and responds carefully, the situation can be salvaged. In practical life, this often appears at the end stage of projects, negotiations, expansions, or conflicts that have gone too far. The answer is not more pressure, but untangling, pausing, and regaining freedom of movement.

overviewZh: 大壮卦，上震下乾，象为雷行天上，阳气盛极而奋发外扬，是力量充盈、行动欲强、志气高昂之时。此卦所说的“壮”，不是一味逞强，也不是情绪化冲撞，而是内在刚健与外在振动相合之后形成的强大推进力。乾为天，代表刚健、自强、原则；震为雷，代表发动、惊醒、突破。二者相叠，表示一个人或一个组织到了可以挺身而出、承担责任、推动局势前进的阶段。但力量越大，越要守正。卦辞仅言“利贞”，正说明大壮之道的关键不在强，而在正；不在快，而在合时。若持强凌弱、冒进躁进，便会像公羊触藩，反受其困。真正的“大壮”，是有边界的力量、有节制的行动、有原则的担当。
overviewEn: Hexagram 34, Da Zhuang, is formed by Thunder above Heaven, presenting the image of vigorous force rising into action. It describes a time when strength, confidence, momentum, and initiative are all abundant. Yet the hexagram does not praise raw aggression. Its core teaching is that great power becomes beneficial only when guided by correctness. Heaven below signifies firmness, integrity, and self-discipline; Thunder above signifies movement, awakening, and decisive action. Together they portray a person, leader, or situation ready to advance boldly. However, when force exceeds propriety, it turns into recklessness. The text repeatedly warns against pushing merely because one can. Great strength must be aligned with timing, moral clarity, and restraint. Thus the judgment says simply that perseverance in what is right is favorable. Da Zhuang is therefore not about domination, but about responsible power: the ability to act strongly without becoming coarse, to break through without losing principle, and to influence events without being trapped by one’s own momentum.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“大壮：利贞”，表面简短，实则点明全卦精神：强盛之时，唯守正而后有利。《彖传》说：“大壮，大者壮也。刚以动，故壮。大壮利贞，大者正也。正大而天地之情可见矣。”所谓“大者壮也”，是说此时阳刚之气充沛，德性、实力、位置都处于上升与扩展之中；“刚以动”则指出，内卦乾之刚健配合外卦震之发动，所以形成巨大的推进力。但《彖传》紧接着把重点落在“正”上，因为力量本身并不天然吉祥，只有符合正道，才真正体现天地生生不息而不相害的规律。大壮不是压人，不是争胜，更不是逞血气，而是在足以作为之时，仍能守礼、守分、守义。故此卦对于君子最重要的提醒是：当你有能力、有气势、有资源时，更要检点自己的方向与方式。能守正，则强而不暴；失其正，则盛势反成危机。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “Great Power. Favorable to be correct and steadfast.” Though brief, it contains the entire doctrine of the hexagram: when strength is abundant, benefit comes only through rectitude. The Tuan Commentary explains, “Great Power means that what is great is strong. Firmness acts, therefore there is power. In Great Power, favorable perseverance means that what is great is correct. When greatness is correct, the nature of Heaven and Earth can be seen.” This commentary is crucial. It first identifies the source of power: inner firmness joined to outer movement. Heaven below provides strength and principle; Thunder above gives activation and momentum. Yet the commentary immediately redirects attention from force to rightness. Power alone is morally neutral. It becomes truly auspicious only when it accords with what is just, timely, and proper. Da Zhuang therefore is not a celebration of conquest or intimidation. It is a lesson in disciplined influence. One may have the capacity to advance, command, or overcome obstacles, but the higher standard is to do so without violating order, dignity, or ethical measure. Greatness that remains correct reveals the living pattern of Heaven and Earth themselves.
imageDetailZh: 象辞曰：“雷在天上，大壮；君子以非礼弗履。”雷本奋发激烈，天本刚健高远，雷行于天上，显示出一种宏大而不可抑止的气势。这种景象提醒人：局势已到可有大作为之时，能量正在聚集并显现，行动有震动四方之效。但君子观此象，并非因此放纵力量，反而更加谨慎于行为尺度，所以说“非礼弗履”。“履”是践行、落脚之意，意即凡不合礼义法度者，即使凭实力做得到，也不应去做。大壮之象最可贵之处，就在于强而能止，盛而不淫，动而有节。真正懂得大壮的人，不把力量用在炫耀与压制上，而用在匡正偏失、承担责任、扶助正道上。故此象既有雷霆之威，也有礼法之绳，二者并行，方成真正可久的壮盛。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Thunder above Heaven: Great Power. Thus the noble person does not tread on what is contrary to propriety.” Thunder in the sky conveys immense energy, resonance, and an expansive force that cannot easily be contained. It suggests a moment when action carries weight, and when initiative can affect the wider environment. Yet the noble person does not interpret this image as permission for excess. On the contrary, the greater the power, the stricter the self-restraint. The phrase “does not tread where there is no propriety” is precise: even if one has the ability to act, not every possible action is worthy of being taken. Da Zhuang teaches that force without ritual measure becomes violence; force governed by principle becomes constructive authority. The noble one therefore uses strength with boundaries, dignity, and moral awareness. The image unites power and restraint: thunder gives energy, heaven gives greatness, and propriety gives direction. Only when all three are present does great strength become lasting and beneficial rather than destructive.
modernApplicationZh: 事业方面，大壮表示你正处在能力突出、资源上升、影响力增强的阶段，适合主动争取项目、承担领导责任、推进难题突破，但切忌因为势头好就压人过甚或决策冒进，尤其要注意制度边界与团队承受度。感情方面，此卦提示关系里容易出现一方气势过强、表达过猛的情况。单身者可积极表达诚意，但不可急于求成；有伴者则需把保护欲、控制欲与真正的关怀区分开来，尊重对方节奏，关系才稳。财运方面，大壮并非偏财投机之卦，更利于凭实力、执行力和组织能力获得进账，如业务扩张、业绩提升、正财增长；若因自信膨胀而重仓冒险，反易受损。健康方面，多与阳气过盛、肝火偏旺、紧张用力过度有关，常见为睡眠不稳、血压波动、肌肉筋骨紧绷；宜运动有度、作息规律、少怒少躁。总之，此卦在现代最重要的启示是：当你有力量时，不要急着证明自己，而要学会把力量放在正确的位置上。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Da Zhuang indicates a phase of rising capability, stronger visibility, and increasing influence. It is favorable for taking initiative, leading difficult projects, making strategic advances, and solving entrenched problems. However, success depends on disciplined execution; if confidence turns into pressure, arrogance, or impulsive expansion, resistance will quickly appear. In relationships, this hexagram warns against overpowering dynamics. A single person may express interest boldly, but should not push for immediate results. In established partnerships, one must distinguish healthy protection from control. Respect for the other person’s pace is essential. Financially, Great Power does not primarily favor speculation. It supports income gained through competence, structured growth, management skill, and sustained effort. Reckless bets made from inflated confidence may lead to unnecessary loss. In health, the hexagram often points to excess yang: tension, irritability, overexertion, muscular tightness, blood pressure fluctuation, or poor sleep caused by mental strain. Moderation in exercise, regular rest, and emotional regulation are helpful. The modern lesson of Da Zhuang is clear: when you possess real power, do not rush to display it; learn instead to place it where it creates value and remains aligned with principle.
historicalStoryZh: 可借商汤伐桀来说明大壮之义。夏桀失道，暴虐无度，天下怨之；商汤则积德修政，安民养众，待时而动。待到人心归附、时势成熟，汤方举义师而伐桀，这不是逞一时武勇，而是以有节制、有名义、有正当性的力量，去匡救天下。此正合大壮“利贞”之旨：强大并不可怕，可怕的是强而失正。若只像匹夫斗狠，虽一时勇猛，终难持久；若能如汤王般以德驭力、以义节兵，则大壮之势可转为开新局、定秩序的正面力量。卦中多次以羝羊触藩为戒，也正反衬出真正的强者不是盲撞，而是知进退、明时势、守道义。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical illustration of Da Zhuang is King Tang’s overthrow of Jie of Xia. Jie had become tyrannical and morally bankrupt, while Tang steadily cultivated virtue, stabilized governance, and won the trust of the people. When the time was ripe and public support had gathered, Tang acted decisively. His campaign was not a display of brute force for its own sake, but the measured use of legitimate strength to restore order. This reflects the central teaching of Great Power: strength becomes beneficial only when aligned with righteousness. Mere ferocity may win a clash but cannot found a lasting order. Tang’s example shows disciplined force under moral purpose—power governed by timing, restraint, and responsibility. That is why Da Zhuang is not simply about being strong; it is about being strong in the right way. The recurring image of the ram caught in the fence warns against blind impact, while Tang exemplifies power used with clarity and justice.
relatedHexagramsNote: 大壮与第33卦“遯”相对观最有意义：遯讲当退则退，以避其锋；大壮讲可进则进，但进中守正。又可参第43卦“夬”，夬重决断去弊，大壮重力量节制。三者合看，可明白进、退、决三种处世节奏。
references: 《彖传》：大壮，大者壮也。刚以动，故壮。大壮利贞，大者正也。正大而天地之情可见矣。
《象传》：雷在天上，大壮；君子以非礼弗履。

judgmentZh: 晋：康侯用锡马蕃庶，昼日三接。
judgmentEn: Progress. The powerful prince is honored with horses in large numbers. In a single day he is granted audience three times.
imageZh: 明出地上，晋；君子以自昭明德。
imageEn: The sun rises over the earth: the image of Progress. Thus the superior man himself brightens his bright virtue.
interpretationZh: 晋卦象征进步晋升，太阳升出地面，光明普照。如康侯受封赏马匹，一日三次觐见，事业蒸蒸日上。
interpretationEn: Jin symbolizes progress and advancement. The sun rises above the earth, spreading light. Like a prince honored with horses and granted triple audience in one day.

lines.textZh: 初六：晋如，摧如，贞吉。罔孚，裕无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Progressing, but turned back. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one meets with no confidence, one should remain calm. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 前进又受挫，坚守正道则吉。不被信任时保持宽裕从容，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing but pushed back. Persevere for fortune. If not trusted, remain calm. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 初六曰“晋如，摧如，贞吉。罔孚，裕无咎。”处晋之初，想前进却先受压抑，故有“摧如”之象。此时最忌因不被信任而急躁自伤，宜守正、宽裕、耐心。虽然一开始外界未必理解你，但只要心量够大，不计一时毁誉，便可免咎。
lines.detailEn: The first line says: “Advancing, yet checked. Perseverance brings good fortune. Though there is no trust, remain generous and there will be no blame.” At the beginning of progress, one wishes to move forward but encounters resistance. The lesson is not to become bitter because recognition is delayed. Hold to what is right, stay patient, and maintain breadth of mind. Temporary doubt from others need not become lasting harm.

lines.textZh: 六二：晋如，愁如，贞吉。受兹介福，于其王母。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Progressing, but in sorrow. Perseverance brings good fortune. Then one obtains great happiness from one's ancestress.
lines.interpretationZh: 前进中有忧愁，坚守正道则吉。从祖母处获得大福。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing in sorrow. Perseverance brings fortune. Great happiness comes from the ancestress.
lines.detailZh: 六二曰“晋如，愁如，贞吉。受兹介福，于其王母。”此爻居中得正，具备前进条件，却仍有忧惧，说明人在上升时常伴随压力与自我怀疑。但因其柔顺中正，终得长者、上位者之庇佑与福泽。此爻提示，稳重谦和比张扬更能带来真正扶持。
lines.detailEn: The second line says: “Advancing, yet with sorrow. Perseverance brings good fortune. One receives great blessing from the queen mother.” Though correctly placed and capable of progress, this line feels worry and pressure. Advancement often comes with emotional weight. Yet because the line is central and proper, it receives support from a higher, nurturing source. Humility and steadiness attract lasting help more effectively than display.

lines.textZh: 六三：众允，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: All are in accord. Remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 众人信任赞同，悔恨消失。
lines.interpretationEn: All are in agreement. Remorse vanishes.
lines.detailZh: 六三曰“众允，悔亡。”走到这一位，关键在于获得众人认可。晋卦重“明”，而明不仅是自己觉得有理，更是能被群体看见其正当性。只要方向正确、做人透明，群体自然愿意支持，过去的疑虑与悔恨也会消散。
lines.detailEn: The third line says: “All approve. Regret disappears.” At this stage, progress becomes secure because it is validated by the community. Jin is about visible brightness, and true brightness can withstand public view. When motives are upright and conduct is transparent, support gathers naturally. With collective trust established, earlier regret and uncertainty fall away.

lines.textZh: 九四：晋如鼫鼠，贞厉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Progress like a hamster. Perseverance brings danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 像鼫鼠一样贪婪地前进，坚持下去有危险。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing like a hamster, hoarding greedily. Perseverance brings danger.
lines.detailZh: 九四曰“晋如鼫鼠，贞厉。”鼫鼠形容贪而怯、善窃而不光明。此爻以阳居阴位，进取之心虽强，却容易手段不正、吃相难看。即使表面仍在“前进”，实则已埋下危险。此爻严厉警告：上升途中一旦失去光明正大，晋就会转为危。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says: “Advancing like a large rat. Even with persistence, there is danger.” The image suggests furtiveness, greed, and advancement through unworthy means. Although movement upward continues, it is tainted by poor character and therefore unstable. This line is a sharp warning: once progress loses transparency and honor, advancement turns dangerous rather than auspicious.

lines.textZh: 六五：悔亡，失得勿恤，往吉，无不利。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Remorse disappears. Take not gain and loss to heart. Undertakings bring good fortune. Everything serves to further.
lines.interpretationZh: 悔恨消失，不必忧虑得失，前往则吉，无往不利。
lines.interpretationEn: Remorse vanishes. Do not worry about gain or loss. Going forward brings fortune in all things.
lines.detailZh: 六五曰“悔亡，失得勿恤，往吉无不利。”这是全卦主爻，柔居尊位，以德服人。到这一层次，不必斤斤计较一时得失，只需把握大方向，继续前行，自然吉而有利。它强调领导者的格局：不为局部损益所困，才能成就更大的晋升与事业。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says: “Regret vanishes. Do not worry about loss or gain. Going forward brings good fortune; nothing is unfavorable.” This is the ruling line of the hexagram. Gentle in manner yet central in authority, it advances through virtue rather than force. The message is to avoid fixation on immediate profit or loss. When one keeps to the larger purpose, progress becomes broadly beneficial and secure.

lines.textZh: 上九：晋其角，维用伐邑，厉吉无咎，贞吝。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Making progress with the horns is permissible only for the purpose of punishing one's own city. To be conscious of danger brings good fortune. No blame. Perseverance brings humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 以角猛进，只可用于征伐自己的城邑。知危则吉，无咎，但固执则有憾。
lines.interpretationEn: Advancing with horns, only to discipline one's own domain. Awareness of danger brings fortune. Stubbornness brings humiliation.
lines.detailZh: 上九曰“晋其角，维用伐邑，厉吉无咎，贞吝。”进到极处，锋芒太露，如角上冲。此时若用于整顿内部、清理局部问题，虽有风险，仍可得吉而无咎；但若固执到底、逞强不止，则会生吝。说明晋到顶点时，更需要克制与收束，知止才能保全成果。
lines.detailEn: The top line says: “Advancing with the horns. It is fitting only to punish one’s own city. There is danger, yet good fortune and no blame; persistence brings humiliation.” At the extreme of progress, force becomes too sharp and visible. Strong action may still be justified for limited internal correction, but not for endless aggressive expansion. The line teaches restraint at the height of success: knowing when to stop preserves what has been gained.

overviewZh: 晋卦，上离下坤，象为明出地上，表示光明渐次升起、德行与事业由内而外地显现。坤为地，厚载而顺；离为火，为光明、文明与洞见。二者相合，意味着一个人经过积累、沉潜与自我修养之后，终于进入可以被看见、被任用、被推举的阶段。晋不是骤然暴起的冲刺，而是在顺势中向上，在明德中前行。卦辞以“康侯用锡马蕃庶，昼日三接”描绘受赏识、得封赏、频繁被接见的荣耀景象，说明此时利于进身、建功、拓展影响力。但晋卦也提醒，越是上升时，越要以光明正大的方式推进，不可急躁邀功，更不可心术不正。真正的“晋”，是让自己的德性、能力与声望同步增长，在众人认可中稳步向前。
overviewEn: Hexagram 35, Jin (Progress), consists of Li above Kun: fire above earth. Its image is brightness rising over the earth, symbolizing visibility, advancement, and the gradual emergence of one’s virtue and capability. Kun represents receptivity, support, and grounded endurance; Li represents light, clarity, and civilization. Together they describe a phase in which long preparation begins to bear visible fruit. Progress here is not reckless acceleration but a steady ascent supported by humility, timing, and inner illumination. The Judgment speaks of a noble lord rewarded with many horses and received repeatedly in broad daylight, suggesting recognition, trust, and opportunity from those in authority. Yet Jin is not merely about promotion or success in worldly terms. It emphasizes that true advancement comes when one’s conduct is clear and one’s influence benefits others. As one rises, public visibility increases, so integrity matters more, not less. The hexagram therefore teaches that progress is most auspicious when it is rooted in sincerity, supported by others, and guided by the desire to manifest one’s brighter nature in the world.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“晋：康侯用锡马蕃庶，昼日三接”，写的是受君王赏识、频繁接见、恩宠日隆之象。其核心在于：当一个人具备明德，又能顺应时势，便容易在群体与权力结构中获得推举。彖传曰：“晋，进也。明出地上，顺而丽乎大明，柔进而上行，是以康侯用锡马蕃庶，昼日三接也。”所谓“进也”，点明本卦主题就是前进、提升、增长；“明出地上”说明前进不是暗中争竞，而是光明显达；“顺而丽乎大明”则强调内在要柔顺中正，外在依附光明正道。六五柔爻居尊位，象征以柔德而得众，以谦和而能进，故得吉。此卦提示：真正可持续的晋升，不靠强取豪夺，而靠才能、德行与时机的配合。若能守正、敬上、亲下、广结人心，则外有提携，内无愧怍，前程自然开阔。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Progress. The noble lord is granted many horses and is received in audience three times in a single day.” This evokes a moment of extraordinary recognition, when one’s abilities are noticed and rewarded by those in power. The Tuanzhuan explains: “Jin means advancing. Brightness appears above the earth. Yielding and attached to great brightness, the soft advances and moves upward. Therefore the noble lord is rewarded with many horses and granted repeated audiences in broad daylight.” This commentary clarifies that progress is not mere ambition. It is advancement made possible because light emerges openly, not secretly, and because the yielding element rises in harmony with what is truly bright. The phrase “the soft advances upward” points especially to the ruling yin line in the fifth place: gentle yet central, modest yet effective. Thus real progress comes through receptivity, moral clarity, and alignment with larger order rather than through aggression. The hexagram teaches that promotion, influence, and success are auspicious when grounded in upright conduct and visible merit. When one serves with sincerity, honors superiors without servility, and wins the confidence of others, recognition comes naturally and progress becomes stable instead of fragile.
imageDetailZh: 象曰：“明出地上，晋；君子以自昭明德。”地上有火，或如旭日出地，原本潜藏的光明开始照耀四方，这正是“晋”的形象。地象征承载、包容与积累，火象征智慧、文明与觉察；火从地上升起，表示内在所蓄的能量终于外化为可见的光彩。君子观此象，不是只求外在显达，而是先“自昭明德”，也就是主动照见并彰显自身本有的善、诚、正与判断力。晋卦的关键并非表演自己，而是把真正的德性活出来，让光明自然照人。象传因此指出，向上发展之前，必须先让内心明净、行为磊落。若内里昏暗，即使一时进取，也容易因名不副实而失势；若能持续修明德行，则其光不炫而自远，进而有本，久而不败。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “Brightness emerges above the earth: Progress. Thus the noble person brings forth and makes bright his luminous virtue.” The picture is vivid: light rising from the earth like dawn, turning what was hidden into something visible and life-giving. Earth signifies support, nourishment, patience, and accumulation; fire signifies clarity, intelligence, and culture. When fire appears above earth, inner substance begins to shine outward. This is why the noble person does not merely chase status but first manifests inner virtue. To “make bright one’s bright virtue” means cultivating honesty, clear judgment, and moral transparency until one’s character itself becomes illuminating. The image warns that outer advancement without inner clarity is unstable. A person may rise quickly yet fall just as quickly if the appearance exceeds the substance. But when progress grows from genuine virtue, influence spreads naturally and enduringly. In this sense, Jin is the movement from hidden potential to visible excellence, from private cultivation to public usefulness, and from inward light to beneficial presence in the wider world.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：晋卦最利于职场晋升、项目推进、品牌曝光与影响力扩大。适合主动汇报成果、争取资源、拓展合作，但要以专业与信誉为基础，切忌急于抢功。感情：关系进入更明朗阶段，适合公开承诺、加深信任、推动关系向前。单身者有机会在社交或工作场合遇到值得发展的人，但需真诚稳定，不可只凭热情冲动。财运：财势有上升机会，尤其利于正财、因职位提升而增收、因声誉提高而得利；偏财则应谨慎，不可因顺势而贪。健康：整体属于阳气渐升、精神转强之象，适合调整作息、增加运动、改善情绪状态；但若忙于进取，需防心火过旺、睡眠不足、焦虑与眼部疲劳。总的来说，晋卦适合“把积累变成成果，把能力变成可见价值”，但一切进展都应建立在明德、节制与长期主义之上。越是在被看见的时候，越要端正初心，这样好运才能由一时之盛转为持续之兴。
modernApplicationEn: Career: Jin is highly favorable for promotion, project advancement, public visibility, and expansion of influence. It is a good time to present achievements, seek support, and build partnerships, but progress should rest on competence and credibility rather than self-display. Relationships: This hexagram points to a clearer and more public phase in love. It supports commitment, deepened trust, and moving the relationship forward. Singles may meet promising partners through work or social settings, but sincerity matters more than excitement alone. Wealth: Financial momentum can improve, especially through regular income, recognition, promotions, or reputation-based opportunities. Speculative gains should be approached cautiously; success should not lead to greed. Health: The general image is one of rising vitality and stronger spirit, making this a good time to regulate routines, exercise, and improve emotional balance. Still, excessive striving may produce stress, overthinking, insomnia, eye strain, or heat-related imbalance. Overall, Jin advises turning accumulated strength into visible value. It favors advancement, but only when that advancement remains disciplined, ethical, and sustainable. The more visible you become, the more essential it is to preserve your original integrity, because lasting progress depends not on a brilliant moment but on brightness that can endure.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周代诸侯受命朝见天子之象来理解晋卦。卦辞中的“康侯”历来多被理解为受王室嘉奖的诸侯，因其有功、有德，故被赐车马、频受接见。这不仅是荣耀，更代表政治秩序中的信任与托付。一个诸侯若平时能安民治国、积德行仁，到了关键时刻，自然会被推举到更重要的位置。晋卦并非单写荣宠，而是在荣宠背后揭示一个古老道理：真正的上升，先来自长期的积累与内在的正当性。德不配位，则恩宠易失；功与德相称，则虽进而不危。古人因此以晋象勉励士君子：想获得朝廷重用，不必巧言令色，而应修己安人，让光明之德自己发出来。
historicalStoryEn: A traditional way to understand Jin is through the image of a regional lord in the Zhou era being honored by the king. The “Kang hou” in the Judgment is often interpreted as a worthy noble who, because of merit and virtue, is granted horses and repeatedly summoned to court. This is not merely ceremonial favor; it signifies political trust and the conferring of greater responsibility. A lord who governed well, brought stability to the people, and cultivated virtue would naturally become a candidate for higher recognition. Thus the hexagram does not celebrate glory for its own sake. It reveals an older principle: lasting elevation depends on accumulated merit and moral legitimacy. If one’s virtue does not match one’s position, honors quickly become dangerous. But when merit and character align, advancement becomes secure. For this reason, Jin served as a moral lesson for scholar-officials: if you wish to be employed and trusted by the court, do not rely on flattery or manipulation; instead, cultivate yourself and let your brightness become evident through service.
relatedHexagramsNote: 晋与明夷互为综卦。明夷是光明受伤、不得显；晋则是光明显现、得以进升。二者共同说明：人生有时当藏光避祸，有时当显德进取。懂得在明夷时守，在晋时进，才是真正顺乎时势的智慧。
references: 《彖传》：晋，进也。明出地上，顺而丽乎大明，柔进而上行……
《象传》：明出地上，晋；君子以自昭明德。
王弼《周易注》相关晋卦注解
孔颖达《周易正义》相关晋卦疏解

judgmentEn: Darkening of the Light. In adversity it furthers one to be persevering.
imageZh: 明入地中，明夷；君子以莅众，用晦而明。
imageEn: The light has sunk into the earth: the image of Darkening of the Light. Thus does the superior man live with the great mass: he veils his light, yet still shines.
interpretationZh: 明夷卦象征光明受损，太阳沉入地下。处于黑暗逆境之中，宜韬光养晦，内心保持光明。
interpretationEn: Ming Yi symbolizes the darkening of the light. The sun has sunk beneath the earth. In dark times, conceal your brilliance outwardly while keeping inner light alive.

lines.textZh: 初九：明夷于飞，垂其翼。君子于行，三日不食。有攸往，主人有言。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Darkening of the light during flight. He lowers his wings. The superior man does not eat for three days on his wanderings. But he has somewhere to go. The host has occasion to gossip.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明受损如鸟飞行垂翼，君子出行三日不食，虽有目的地，但主人有闲言。
lines.interpretationEn: The light darkens in flight; wings droop. The wanderer goes hungry for three days. He has a destination, but the host gossips.
lines.detailZh: “明夷于飞，垂其翼。”初九居下卦之始，有欲飞之志，却逢黑暗之世，只能收敛羽翼。君子出行，甚至到“三日不食”的艰难程度，仍要离开是非之地，因此还会招来主人的责难。此爻强调：在局势初坏之时，识时离场比硬撑更明智。
lines.detailEn: “Darkening of the Light in flight; he lowers his wings.” At the beginning of the hexagram, one still has vigor and aspiration, yet the times are hostile. The noble person must restrain movement and depart from danger, even at the cost of hardship, hunger, and criticism from others. The line teaches that early withdrawal from a corrupt situation may be wiser than stubborn endurance.

lines.textZh: 六二：明夷，夷于左股，用拯马壮，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Darkening of the light injures him in the left thigh. He gives aid with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 光明受损伤及左腿，用壮马之力救援，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: The light is wounded in the left thigh. Rescue comes with the strength of a horse. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “明夷，夷于左股，用拯马壮，吉。”六二柔顺得中，虽受伤于左股，行动受限，但仍有援救之机。借助强壮之马，象征依靠外力、资源或贵人脱困。此爻之吉，在于虽伤不乱，及时求助，故能保全。
lines.detailEn: “Darkening of the Light; wounded in the left thigh. He uses a strong horse for rescue—good fortune.” This line suggests injury that impairs movement, yet not total ruin. Help remains available through strength, allies, or timely support. Its good fortune lies in not collapsing under harm, but accepting practical assistance and preserving oneself.

lines.textZh: 九三：明夷于南狩，得其大首，不可疾贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: Darkening of the light during the hunt in the south. Their great leader is captured. One must not expect perseverance too hastily.
lines.interpretationZh: 在南方狩猎时光明受损，擒获大首领，但不可急于求成。
lines.interpretationEn: During the southern hunt, the great leader is captured. Do not expect quick results.
lines.detailZh: “明夷于南狩，得其大首，不可疾贞。”九三刚而居动位，有能力采取行动，在黑暗中反击并有所斩获，“得其大首”象征擒获关键问题或首恶之人。但爻辞又戒“不可疾贞”，说明即便有成果，也不可急进冒进，仍须守正而缓行。
lines.detailEn: “Darkening of the Light during the southern hunt; one gains the great head. It is not suitable to press on in haste.” Here there is strength and an opportunity to strike effectively at the root of disorder. Yet success must not lead to overconfidence. Even after gaining a decisive advantage, one should remain cautious, principled, and free from rash momentum.

lines.textZh: 六四：入于左腹，获明夷之心，出于门庭。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: He penetrates the left side of the belly. One gets at the very heart of the darkening of the light, and leaves gate and courtyard.
lines.interpretationZh: 深入左腹，洞悉明夷的核心，然后离开门庭。
lines.interpretationEn: Penetrating the left belly, one grasps the heart of the darkness and departs through the gate.
lines.detailZh: “入于左腹，获明夷之心，于出门庭。”六四已近上体，深入险境内部，因而看清黑暗真正的用心。“左腹”有深入内里之象，既是接近危险，也是洞察核心。明白局势本质后，便应果断离开门庭，不再迟疑。此爻重在看透与抽身。
lines.detailEn: “He enters the left belly and gains the heart of Darkening of the Light; then he leaves the courtyard gate.” This line depicts penetrating into the interior of a dark situation and understanding its true motives. Once the essence of danger is seen, withdrawal becomes possible and necessary. The wisdom here lies in insight first, then timely exit.

lines.textZh: 六五：箕子之明夷，利贞。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi. Perseverance furthers.
lines.interpretationZh: 如箕子般韬光养晦，坚守正道则有利。
lines.interpretationEn: Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi. Conceal your brilliance. Perseverance furthers.
lines.detailZh: “箕子之明夷，利贞。”六五居尊位而以柔中处之，是明夷卦中最具德性的代表。箕子面对暴政，不与其同恶，也不轻率赴死，而是以隐忍守志来保存道义。此爻说明，身居险中，最可贵的是内心贞正不改，哪怕外在不得不晦藏。
lines.detailEn: “The Darkening of the Light of Ji Zi. Perseverance is favorable.” This is the exemplary line of the hexagram. Ji Zi survives tyranny not by compromise of principle, nor by impulsive self-destruction, but by preserving moral clarity under concealment. The line honors inward fidelity when outer expression must be restrained.

lines.textZh: 上六：不明晦，初登于天，后入于地。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Not light but darkness. First he climbed up to heaven, then he plunged into the depths of the earth.
lines.interpretationZh: 不是光明而是黑暗。先登上天空，后坠入地下，由盛转衰。
lines.interpretationEn: Not light but darkness. First ascending to heaven, then plunging into the earth. From height to ruin.
lines.detailZh: “不明晦，初登于天，后入于地。”上六为明夷之极，象征昏暗达到顶点。开始时如升于天，极盛极高，后来却坠入于地，说明失德的光终将反噬自身。对个人而言，此爻也警示：若完全失去光明与分寸，便会由盛转衰，堕入幽暗。
lines.detailEn: “Not bright but dark. At first he ascends to heaven, later he enters the earth.” This top line shows darkness at its extreme. What once rose high now falls into the ground, suggesting the collapse of false brilliance or power cut off from virtue. For individuals, it warns that when clarity is fully lost, decline follows even after apparent elevation.

overviewZh: 明夷卦，上坤下离，象为光明沉入地中，故名“明夷”，意为光被伤、才被掩。此卦所呈现的，不是单纯的衰败，而是在黑暗环境中保存火种、隐忍守正的智慧。离为明，坤为顺，当文明受困于昏乱之世，最重要的不是逞强争胜，而是知时而退、藏锋守志。它提醒人们：当外部秩序不利、真诚与能力反被压制时，仍应守住内在原则，以柔顺外示、清明内持。明夷并不鼓励消沉，相反，它强调在逆境中保全自己、等待时机、以长期定力穿越黑暗，是一种极高层次的处世与修身之道。
overviewEn: Hexagram 36, Darkening of the Light, is formed by Earth above Fire. Its image is of brightness sinking beneath the earth, suggesting that light is wounded, obscured, or forced into concealment. This hexagram does not simply describe defeat; rather, it teaches how wisdom and integrity survive in hostile conditions. Fire represents clarity and civilization, while Earth signifies receptivity and restraint. When one’s light cannot shine openly, the proper response is not reckless confrontation but disciplined concealment, inner steadiness, and loyalty to principle. Ming Yi speaks to times when talent, honesty, or goodness are misunderstood or even attacked. In such periods, survival itself becomes meaningful. One preserves the inner flame, acts with caution, and waits for the right season. The hexagram therefore offers a profound philosophy of endurance: true light may be hidden, but it is not extinguished.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞“明夷：利艰贞”，指出此卦之道不在顺境求进，而在艰难中守贞。所谓“利”，不是立刻得势得利，而是在危局中坚持正道反而最有价值。《彖传》说：“明入地中，明夷。内文明而外柔顺，以蒙大难，文王以之。利艰贞，晦其明也。内难而能正其志，箕子以之。”这段话点明明夷的核心：内在仍然文明清醒，外在却表现柔顺低调，以避大难。周文王被囚而不失其德，箕子佯狂受辱而不改其志，都是“晦其明”的典范。因此，明夷不是教人放弃理想，而是在不利局势下，把锋芒收藏，把志向守住。真正的贞正，不一定外显强硬，而是即便处在黑暗压迫之中，内心依旧不偏不倚，不失其明。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “Darkening of the Light. It is favorable to be persevering in hardship.” The key idea is that benefit does not come from outward success, but from maintaining righteousness under pressure. The Tuan Commentary explains: “Brightness sinks into the earth: this is Darkening of the Light. Inwardly civilized, outwardly gentle and yielding, one endures great difficulty—King Wen embodied this. ‘Favorable in hardship and perseverance’ means hiding one’s brightness. In inner distress yet able to keep one’s will correct—Ji Zi embodied this.” This commentary reveals the heart of the hexagram. One must preserve inner clarity while adopting an outwardly modest, restrained, even obscure manner. It is not a teaching of surrender, but of strategic concealment. King Wen, imprisoned yet morally unbroken, and Ji Zi, feigning madness to survive tyranny, represent this ideal. Real integrity in dark times is not loud heroism; it is the ability to protect one’s light without betraying one’s center.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“明入地中，明夷；君子以莅众，用晦而明。”火本应照耀四方，如今却入于地中，象征光明被遮蔽、贤智不容于时。君子见此，不是愤而妄动，而是懂得“用晦而明”——表面收敛，内心通达；外在不争，实则洞察。所谓“莅众”，说明即便处于晦暗局面，领导众人时也不能只凭锋芒与高调，而应以含蓄、谨慎、包容去维系秩序。明夷之象告诉人们，真正成熟的光明，不一定总是炽烈外放；有时它表现为沉潜、忍耐、低姿态中的清醒。这种将光藏于内、把锋化为德的能力，正是逆境中的大智慧。
imageDetailEn: The Image Commentary says, “Brightness enters the earth: Darkening of the Light. Thus the noble person, in governing the masses, uses obscurity yet remains bright.” Fire normally illuminates openly, but here it disappears beneath the earth, symbolizing a time when clarity and virtue cannot be displayed without danger. The noble person therefore does not react impulsively. Instead, they practice being outwardly muted while inwardly lucid. “Using obscurity yet remaining bright” means concealing brilliance without losing discernment. The phrase about governing others adds an important dimension: leadership in troubled times is sustained not by display, but by restraint, patience, and subtle guidance. Ming Yi teaches that mature light is not always radiant on the surface. Sometimes true illumination survives as quiet intelligence, hidden strength, and ethical steadiness beneath conditions of darkness.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代生活中，明夷卦尤其适用于环境复杂、局势压抑、个人能力难以施展的阶段。事业上，若遭遇组织内耗、上级昏聩或同侪掣肘，不宜硬碰硬，应先保全位置与实力，低调积累，等待局势转机。感情上，明夷提示关系中可能存在误解、冷淡或一方被压抑的情形，此时应先看清对方与环境，而非急于摊牌；能守住真心，也要守住边界。财运上，不利冒进投资和高调扩张，更适合现金流管理、风险控制与保守布局，避免因外部环境不稳而受损。健康上，多对应情绪压抑、睡眠失衡、肝火郁结或长期精神消耗，宜减压、静养、规律作息。整体而言，明夷不是“做不了事”，而是提醒人们在灰暗阶段换一种做法：先藏锋、先自保、先养势，等到时机回暖，再让真正的光重新照出来。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 36 is especially relevant when the environment is suppressive, politically difficult, or unreceptive to one’s abilities. In career matters, if one faces organizational dysfunction, poor leadership, jealousy, or hidden opposition, direct confrontation is often unwise. It is better to protect one’s position, build quietly, and wait for a more favorable opening. In relationships, Darkening of the Light may indicate emotional misunderstanding, coldness, or a situation in which one person feels unseen or constrained. The advice is to preserve sincerity, but also maintain boundaries and avoid dramatic escalation before seeing clearly. Financially, this is not an ideal time for aggressive speculation or showy expansion. Conservative planning, cash-flow awareness, and risk control are preferable. In health, the hexagram often points to emotional suppression, fatigue, sleep imbalance, or stress-related depletion; rest and inner regulation are essential. Overall, Ming Yi does not mean paralysis. It means changing strategy in a dark season: hide the blade, preserve energy, and let timing ripen before acting openly again.
historicalStoryZh: 明夷卦常以周文王与箕子的故事为注脚。殷商末年，纣王无道，忠良受害。西伯昌即后来的周文王，被囚于羑里，身处危境，却并未因屈辱而失德，反而推演易道，蓄养天下之志，这是“内文明而外柔顺，以蒙大难”的典型。箕子则是商朝宗室贤臣，面对暴政，不愿同流合污，又无法直行其道，便披发佯狂，以晦藏明，保全性命与道统。二人一个在囚禁中守志，一个在乱世中藏明，都说明真正的智慧不是盲目逞强，而是在黑暗时代让精神不灭、道义不断。明夷因此具有浓厚的历史感：当世界昏暗，君子先护其明，而后待时。
historicalStoryEn: Hexagram 36 is traditionally illuminated by the stories of King Wen of Zhou and Ji Zi. Near the end of the Shang dynasty, tyranny prevailed and loyal ministers were endangered. Ji Chang, later known as King Wen, was imprisoned at Youli. Though humiliated and constrained, he did not lose virtue or vision; instead, he cultivated inner clarity and deepened the wisdom later associated with the Changes. This exemplifies being inwardly civilized and outwardly compliant while enduring great hardship. Ji Zi, a noble kinsman of the Shang house, faced the corruption of King Zhou and chose neither open collaboration nor reckless martyrdom. He feigned madness, hiding his brilliance in order to preserve both life and principle. One endured captivity, the other concealed wisdom in chaos, yet both embodied the same teaching: in dark times, the highest courage may lie in protecting the light rather than displaying it.
relatedHexagramsNote: 明夷与第35卦“晋”互为对照：晋是光明显进，明夷是光明受伤；一盛一晦，提示进退有时。又可与第47卦“困”参看，困重在受限，明夷重在藏明；困求通，明夷求守。理解这几卦的转换，有助把握顺逆中的行动尺度。
references: 《彖传》：明入地中，明夷。内文明而外柔顺，以蒙大难，文王以之。利艰贞，晦其明也。内难而能正其志，箕子以之。
《象传》：明入地中，明夷；君子以莅众，用晦而明。
